name: Schlieffen Plan — Western Offensive 1914
detail: Germany's pre-WWI strategy to knock out France in 6 weeks via massive right-wing sweep through Belgium, then pivot east against Russia. Moltke the Younger modified the plan; French resistance at the Marne (Sept 1914) stalled the advance and locked both sides into trench warfare for four years.

name: Fall Weiss — Invasion of Poland, September 1939
detail: Germany launched the world's first Blitzkrieg operation against Poland on September 1, 1939, with 1.5 million troops, 2,500 tanks, and 1,900 aircraft. Warsaw fell October 6; Poland divided between Germany and Soviet Union under Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact. This triggered WWII.

name: Fall Gelb — Conquest of France, May–June 1940
detail: Germany bypassed the Maginot Line through the Ardennes with Army Group A (Manstein plan), encircling Allied forces at Dunkirk. France fell in 46 days — stunning reversal of WWI. Paris occupied June 14, 1940; armistice signed June 22. Revenged 1871 French humiliation.

name: Operation Barbarossa — Invasion of Soviet Union, 1941
detail: Germany's June 22, 1941 invasion of the USSR: largest land operation in history, 3.8 million Axis troops on 2,900 km front. Initial gains of 1,000+ km by autumn; German advance halted at Moscow, Leningrad, and Rostov. Strategic miscalculation that ultimately decided WWII outcome.

name: Operation Michael — Spring Offensive 1918
detail: Germany's last major offensive of WWI (March 21, 1918) using Sturmtruppen infiltration tactics. Advanced up to 65 km — largest territorial gains since 1914. Allied line held under Foch's unified command; German army exhausted its reserves. Collapse followed by summer 1918.

name: Battle of Stalingrad — Turning Point 1942–1943
detail: Germany's 6th Army (330,000 troops) encircled and destroyed at Stalingrad by Soviet Operation Uranus (Nov 1942). Paulus surrendered February 2, 1943 with 91,000 survivors. Psychological and strategic turning point of WWII's Eastern Front; Germany never successfully attacked again.

name: Ardennes Offensive — Battle of the Bulge 1944
detail: Germany's last major Western Front offensive (December 16, 1944): 200,000 troops, 600 tanks aimed to split Allied lines and capture Antwerp. Allied resistance (Bastogne, Patton relief) halted German advance by January 1945. Germany expended irreplaceable reserves; Allied victory accelerated.

name: Allied Strategic Bombing Campaign 1942–1945
detail: RAF and USAAF dropped 1.35 million tons of bombs on Germany 1942–45 (Operation Overlord supporting). Key raids: Cologne (May 1942, first 1,000-bomber raid), Hamburg firestorm (July 1943, 37,000 dead), Dresden (Feb 1945, ~25,000 dead). Reduced German industrial output but did not break civilian morale as expected.
